full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: In DP-means, why does using squared Euclidean distance imply an isotropic Gaussian cluster assumption? Which equation in the paper does this come from?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Linked Euclidean distance to Eq. 9 and explained that Voronoi partitions only correspond to spherical Gaussians.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified by re-reading Eq. 9 and Section 4's derivation — confirmed the covariance is sigma^2 I.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What assumption does a single global lambda make about the clusters in the dataset, and when would this assumption fail?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Articulated the uniform density/scale assumption and suggested a mixed-scale cluster dataset as a violation.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked Section 3 of the paper — no per-cluster lambda is defined, confirming this is a real assumption.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What does sigma squared going to zero assume about cluster overlap in the DP-means derivation, and when would this break down?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Explained that hard assignments are only valid when clusters are well-separated relative to their variance.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Located Theorem 1 in Section 4 and confirmed the sigma^2 -> 0 limit is the formal basis of the algorithm.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Finding paper references for each assumption
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For each of the three DP-means assumptions (spherical clusters, global lambda, small-variance asymptotics), what is the most specific section, equation, or theorem in the Kulis Jordan 2012 paper that supports each assumption?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Identified Eq. 9 for assumption 1, Section 3 for assumption 2, and Theorem 1 Section 4 for assumption 3.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified each reference by locating the corresponding passage in the paper.

top_5_prompts.prompt: In DP-means, why does using squared Euclidean distance imply an isotropic Gaussian cluster assumption? Which equation in the paper does this come from?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the most technically precise assumption and required understanding the link between Euclidean distance and Gaussian covariance.

top_5_prompts.prompt: What does sigma squared going to zero assume about cluster overlap in the DP-means derivation, and when would this break down?
top_5_prompts.why_important: The small-variance assumption is the deepest assumption in the paper and required tracing the derivation in Section 4.

top_5_prompts.prompt: What assumption does a single global lambda make about the clusters in the dataset, and when would this assumption fail?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Practical violation scenarios for lambda uniformity are important for Part C questions about real-world limitations.

top_5_prompts.prompt: For each of the three DP-means assumptions (spherical clusters, global lambda, small-variance asymptotics), what is the most specific section, equation, or theorem in the Kulis Jordan 2012 paper that supports each assumption?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Ensuring every assumption has a traceable paper reference is required by the exam rubric.
